items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce
items.summary: This update resolves critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source. Successful exploitation could lead to arbitrary code execution, security feature bypass, and privilege escalation.

items.title: Unauthenticated PHP object injection in Mirasvit Cache Warmer
items.summary: Sansec discovered a critical unauthenticated PHP object injection vulnerability in Mirasvit Cache Warmer, a Magento extension, allowing remote code execution via a crafted cookie. Mirasvit released a patched version (1.11.12) on May 25, 2026.

items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce
items.summary: This bulletin fixes 15 CVEs in Adobe Commerce, Adobe Commerce B2B, and Magento Open Source, including critical stored XSS (CVE-2026-34686) and path traversal (CVE-2026-34653) vulnerabilities that could lead to arbitrary code execution or file system write.

items.title: Unrestricted file upload in Magento and Adobe Commerce REST API
items.summary: Sansec discovered PolyShell, a critical flaw in Magento's REST API that allows unauthenticated attackers to upload executable files using a polyglot technique. Adobe released Magento 2.4.9 with a fix, but older supported versions remain vulnerable.

items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce
items.summary: This critical security update addresses multiple vulnerabilities in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source, including CVE-2026-21290 (Stored XSS leading to privilege escalation) and others that could result in security feature bypass, arbitrary code execution, and arbitrary file system read.

items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce
items.summary: This update resolves critical and important vulnerabilities in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source, including a stored cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability via server-side template injection (CVE-2025-47110) and a reflected XSS/one-click account takeover issue.

items.title: Arbitrary Code Execution vulnerability (CosmicSting)
items.summary: This critical vulnerability, also known as CosmicSting, was exploited in the wild in limited attacks targeting Adobe Commerce merchants. It could lead to arbitrary code execution, security feature bypass, and privilege escalation. An isolated patch was provided on June 27, 2024.

items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce
items.summary: This critical security update addresses vulnerabilities in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source that could lead to security feature bypass, privilege escalation, and application denial-of-service.

items.title: Unauthenticated Remote Code Execution (SessionReaper)
items.summary: Named 'SessionReaper' by Sansec, this critical flaw allows customer account takeover and unauthenticated remote code execution under certain conditions. Adobe released an emergency fix, and mass attacks were observed in October 2025.

items.title: Improper Access Control vulnerability
items.summary: Adobe Commerce versions 2.4.7-beta1, 2.4.7-p3, 2.4.6-p8, 2.4.5-p10, 2.4.4-p11 and earlier are affected by an Improper Access Control vulnerability that could result in Privilege escalation.
